audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Deuteronomy / Devarim divorce, pledge, kidnapping, wage, individual-liability, and gleaning law, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for divorce certificate and remarriage restriction, newlywed exemption, millstone and garment pledges, kidnapping, skin-disease instruction, wage payment, individual responsibility, and gleaning for vulnerable residents.

verses.restored: If a man takes a woman and becomes her husband, and it is that she finds no favor in his eyes because he found in her nakedness/indecency of a thing, and he writes for her a scroll of cutting-off/divorce, puts it in her hand, and sends her from his house,
and she goes out from his house and goes and becomes wife of another man,
and latter husband hates her and writes for her a scroll of cutting-off, puts it in her hand, and sends her from his house, or latter husband who took her as wife dies,

then her first husband who sent her away may not return to take her to become his wife after she has been made unclean, for that is an abomination before YHWH; you shall not cause land to sin that YHWH your Divine gives you as inheritance.
When a man takes a new wife, he shall not go out with host, and no matter shall pass upon him; free he shall be for his house one year, and he shall gladden wife whom he took.
One shall not take a handmill or upper millstone as pledge, for he takes a soul/life as pledge.
If a man is found stealing a soul/person from his brothers, children of Israel, and he treats him as merchandise or sells him, then that thief shall die; so you shall remove evil from your midst.
Guard yourself concerning affliction of scale-disease, to keep greatly and do according to all Levitical priests instruct you; as I commanded them, you shall keep to do.
Remember what YHWH your Divine did to Miriam on way when you came out from Egypt.
When you lend your brother any loan, you shall not enter his house to take his pledge.
Outside you shall stand, and man to whom you lend shall bring pledge out to you.
If he is poor man, you shall not lie down with his pledge.
You shall surely return pledge to him when sun goes down, so he may lie in his garment and bless you; and it shall be righteousness for you before YHWH your Divine.
You shall not oppress a hired worker who is poor and needy, from your brothers or from your sojourner who is in your land within your gates.
In his day you shall give his wage, and sun shall not go down upon it, for he is poor and sets his soul/life upon it; lest he cry against you to YHWH and sin/misalignment be in you.
Fathers shall not be put to death for children, and children shall not be put to death for fathers; each man shall be put to death for his own sin/misalignment.
You shall not bend judgment of sojourner or orphan, and you shall not take garment of widow as pledge.
You shall remember you were a slave in Egypt and YHWH your Divine redeemed you from there; therefore I command you to do this thing.

When you reap your harvest in field and forget a sheaf in field, you shall not return to take it; it shall be for sojourner, orphan, and widow, so YHWH your Divine may bless you in all work of your hands.
When you beat your olive tree, you shall not go over branches after yourself; it shall be for sojourner, orphan, and widow.
When you gather grapes of your vineyard, you shall not glean after yourself; it shall be for sojourner, orphan, and widow.
You shall remember you were a slave in land of Egypt; therefore I command you to do this thing.

translation_notes: The divorce law assumes male-initiated divorce and gives the woman a written document, but it does not establish equal marital agency. The phrase ervat davar is debated and should not be converted into unrestricted fault-finding.
The remarriage prohibition regulates the first husband's claim after a subsequent marriage; it must not stigmatize divorced or remarried people.
The newlywed exemption protects time for a new household and obligates the husband to bring joy to his wife.
Taking a millstone is forbidden because it seizes the debtor's means of making food and therefore takes life as collateral.
Kidnapping a person for exploitation or sale is condemned, though broader slavery remains elsewhere in the legal corpus.
Scale-disease language is ritual/medical tradition and must not stigmatize skin conditions or blame illness on wrongdoing.
Same-day wages recognize that workers' lives depend upon prompt payment.
Individual-liability law rejects execution of relatives for another person's offense.
Gleaning deliberately leaves productive value in field, olive tree, and vineyard for immigrant, orphan, and widow.
safety_note: Do not use Deuteronomy 24 to shame divorced people, trap anyone in unsafe marriage, exploit workers, seize essential household tools, kidnap or traffic people, stigmatize illness, punish families for another person's conduct, or deny immigrants and widows justice. Center prompt wages, protected collateral, individual responsibility, and food left deliberately for vulnerable neighbors.
